text:
Once upon a time, in a sleepy town nestled between valleys, lived a boy named Toby. He loved nature. His favorite tree was an ancient oak in the town square. Every summer weekend, he would climb up its gnarled branches.

So one autumn afternoon, when leaves began falling faster than usual, Toby felt melancholy. He stayed home that weekend instead of exploring outdoors.

Days passed, and Toby's cheeks grew pinker. Then he noticed something.

Behind his house, among fallen apples and leaves, there lay a peculiar doll. Its eyes seemed to follow him. Toby picked it up.

Suddenly, it spoke. "Thank you for finding me."

Toby looked puzzled. "Really? You talk?"

"Yes." The doll smiled mysteriously. "Tell me your dreams."

Dreams? Toby blinked. "Hmm..."

Dreaming of wild oceans and distant stars. An adventure across beaches where waves sang songs.

The doll nudged gently. "Go chase those dreams!"

Toby nodded
